features.description: Repo contains grounded product, architecture, data-model, diagram, and task docs for the intent-first route-based rebuild with exact-member and grouped-preset URIs
steps: Open the PRD and verify the one-URI-one-fixed-source-shape rule is defined
Open the architecture note and verify discovery-published exact-member and grouped-preset choices plus runtime/session separation are documented
Open the data-model note and verify the minimal durable schema plus PK/FK plan are defined
Open the diagram and verify attach-existing-session and grouped-preset expansion are documented
Open the task list and verify the future implementation order is documented

features.description: The design docs define RGBD depth choices as separate discovery-visible outputs when D2C changes delivered caps
steps: Open the grouped-source writeup
Verify `orbbec/depth/400p_30` and `orbbec/depth/480p_30` are documented as separate discovery choices
Verify `orbbec/preset/480p_30` is documented as the proven grouped preset choice
Open the PRD and verify route expectations no longer choose alignment implicitly
Open the REST reference and verify grouped preset binds use `route_grouped` instead of route-side pairing helpers

features.description: The design docs state that grouped-source requests must resolve to one compatible grouped runtime or reject, without bind-time override of URI meaning
steps: Open the PRD and verify the grouped runtime rule is documented
Open the architecture note and verify grouped conflicts reject instead of silently mutating runtime meaning
Open the REST reference and verify normal use stays backend-fixed per discovered URI

features.description: The design docs separate derived URI identity from durable delivery intent and use one app-source surface for connect and attach
steps: Open the data-model note and verify `uri` is derived while `delivery_name` is inferred then stored durably on `app_sources` and `sessions`
Open the REST reference and verify `POST /api/apps/{id}/sources` accepts exactly one of `input` or `session_id` and exactly one of `route` or `route_grouped`
Open the PRD and verify local SDK attach is IPC-only in v1
Open the PRD or REST reference and verify future remote or LAN RTSP consumption is documented as a separate path

features.description: The design docs keep channel selection in the URI path and define grouped preset fan-out through `route_grouped` instead of an extra SDK-only frame-merge layer
steps: Open the grouped-source writeup and verify `/channel/<name>` is preferred over query-param selection for usage reasons
Open the PRD and verify grouped preset URIs are documented alongside ordinary routes
Open the architecture diagram and verify grouped preset expansion feeds ordinary route callbacks without an extra SDK-only frame-merge layer

features.description: Backend persists apps and lists them after backend restart
steps: Create an app through the REST API
Verify the app appears in GET /api/apps
Restart the backend with the same database
Verify the app still appears in GET /api/apps

features.description: App can declare a purpose route named yolov5 with a video expectation
steps: Create an app
Create a route named yolov5 with media expectation video
List routes for the app
Verify yolov5 appears with the declared expectation

features.description: App can declare a route named orbbec/color with a video expectation
steps: Create an app
Create a route named orbbec/color with media expectation video
List routes for the app
Verify orbbec/color appears with the declared expectation

features.description: App can declare a route named orbbec/depth with a depth expectation
steps: Create an app
Create a route named orbbec/depth with media expectation depth
List routes for the app
Verify orbbec/depth appears with the declared expectation

features.description: User can browse discovery-published URIs knowing that each URI represents one fixed published source shape
steps: Open the frontend or device catalog API
Inspect listed URI entries
Verify each listed URI corresponds to one exact member or one grouped preset bundle
Verify no route expectation is required to disambiguate the source identity

features.description: RGBD discovery exposes separate depth outputs when D2C changes delivered caps
steps: Open the device catalog API for an RGBD device
Verify separate exact URIs are listed for `orbbec/depth/400p_30` and `orbbec/depth/480p_30`
Verify the grouped preset URI `orbbec/preset/480p_30` is also listed when enabled for that device
Verify their metadata shows distinct delivered caps or capture policy

features.description: Stereo or dual-eye devices can expose channel-disambiguated exact URIs
steps: Open the device catalog API for a stereo device
Verify exact URIs exist for left and right channels
Verify the route layer is not required to guess the channel

features.description: Injecting an exact video URI with route yolov5 routes callbacks to the app
steps: Create an app and declare route yolov5
Inject a compatible exact video URI with route yolov5
Verify the backend creates a logical session
Verify the SDK receives callbacks on the declared route

features.description: Injecting an exact depth URI with route orbbec/depth routes callbacks
steps: Create an app and declare route orbbec/depth
Inject a compatible URI such as insightos://host/desk-rgbd/orbbec/depth/480p_30 with route orbbec/depth
Verify the backend records the resolved exact stream identity
Verify the SDK receives callbacks on the declared route

features.description: Injecting a grouped Orbbec preset URI with route_grouped orbbec activates ordinary color and depth routes together
steps: Create an app and declare routes orbbec/color and orbbec/depth
Inject a compatible URI such as insightos://host/desk-rgbd/orbbec/preset/480p_30 with route_grouped orbbec
Verify the backend records one grouped source bind with resolved member mappings
Verify the SDK receives callbacks on both declared routes without an extra SDK-only frame-merge layer

features.description: A route can attach to an already-running IPC-capable direct logical session through the app-source surface
steps: Create a direct session through POST /api/sessions from a locally resolved `insightos://` URI so delivery infers to `ipc`
Create an app and declare a compatible route
Create one app-source bind using the existing `session_id` plus `route`
Verify the app receives callbacks on the attached route without recreating the stream

features.description: A grouped target can attach to an already-running grouped logical session through the same app-source surface
steps: Create a grouped direct session through POST /api/sessions from a locally resolved grouped preset URI so delivery infers to `ipc`
Create an app and declare routes such as orbbec/color and orbbec/depth
Create one app-source bind using the existing `session_id` plus `route_grouped`
Verify the app receives callbacks on both declared routes without separately managing `/color` and `/depth`

features.description: The same URI and delivery intent can be used in multiple places while delivering the same frame sequence or fixed grouped member set
steps: Bind the same URI and delivery intent into multiple consumers
Inspect runtime status
Verify shared delivery reuse is visible when applicable
Verify consumers observe the same frame sequence

features.description: The same URI with different delivery intents remains distinct delivery sessions while sharing capture when possible
steps: Create one `ipc` session and one `rtsp` session for the same exact source
Inspect runtime status
Verify separate delivery intents exist
Verify shared capture reuse is visible

features.description: Source connection rejects a URI whose resolved source metadata does not satisfy the route expectation
steps: Create an app and declare a route with a depth expectation
Inject a normal video URI into that route
Verify the backend rejects the request with a compatibility error

features.description: Apps, routes, and sources survive backend restart while runtime state is normalized to stopped
steps: Create an app, route, and source
Restart the backend with the same database
List the app sources
Verify the source is still present and its state is stopped

features.description: A route or grouped route target can change from one URI or attached session to another at runtime
steps: Create an app, route, and initial source binding
Call the route rebind API with a replacement `input` or `session_id`
Verify durable state points at the new binding
Verify obsolete route-owned runtime is stopped when appropriate

features.description: Browser UI supports discover-create-route-connect-start-stop flow end to end
steps: Open the frontend
Inspect listed exact URIs
Create an app
Create a route
Connect a listed exact-member or grouped-preset URI to that route or grouped route target
Start the source
Stop the source
Verify status updates are visible in the UI
